title: Seismic Structure and Tectonics of the North-Central Chilean Subduction Zone Along the Copiapó Ridge From Amphibious Seismic Refraction Tomography and Local Seismicity
abstract: Wide-angle seismic refraction and reflection data were recorded on 36 OBS/OBH and 10 seismic land stations along an approximately E-W oriented 445 km long transect that aligns with the Copiapó Ridge off Chile's coastline during R/V SONNE cruise SO297. The study investigates structural controls on the north-central Chilean subduction zone at ~27°S using amphibious seismic refraction tomography and local seismicity. Oceanic crust thickness is 6–7 km with P-wave velocities between 3.0 and 7.3 km/s. The subduction angle increases gradually from 12° in the forearc to 22° at greater depths. Three distinct seismicity bands are identified aligned with plate structures, with the largest detected event reaching magnitude 5.9 in the deepest zone.

location.description: Offshore north-central Chile (~27°S) along the Copiapó Ridge transect

data.name: Wide-angle OBS/OBH seismic refraction data, SO297
data.description: Seismic refraction data recorded on 36 OBS/OBH and 10 land stations along 445 km E-W transect at ~27°S. Data used for P-wave travel time tomography.

data.name: SO297 multibeam bathymetry raw data (Kongsberg EM 122)
SO297 water column raw data (Kongsberg EM 122)
Seismic processed data (Ocean Bottom Seismometer working area dataset) of RV SONNE during cruise SO297, Copiapó Ridge, Chile
data.description: The data comprises processed active-source seismic data acquired with ocean bottom seismometer (OBS). The data were collected at the Copiapó Ridge during expedition SO297 with the research vessel RV SONNE (doi:10.48433/cr_so297) offshore Copiapó, north-central Chile (map for further details). The entire data set consists of 26 OBS stations. Seismic data were shot across the OBS along one profile. Each OBS consists of 4 channels (channel 1: hydrophone; channel 2: horizontal component 1; channel 3: horizontal component 2; channel 4: vertical component).

data.name: Seismic processed data (Ocean Bottom Hydrophone working area dataset) of RV SONNE during cruise SO297, Copiapó Ridge, Chile
data.description: The data comprise processed active-source seismic data acquired with ocean bottom hydrophone (OBH). The data were collected at the Copiapó Ridge during expedition SO297 with the research vessel RV SONNE (doi:10.48433/cr_so297) offshore Copiapó, north-central Chile (map for further details). The entire data set consists of 10 OBH stations. Seismic data were shot across the OBH along one profile. Each OBH consists of 1 channel (hydrophone).

data.name: Seismic processed data (Onshore Seismometer working area dataset) of project PISAGUA, Atacama Desert near Copiapó, Chile
data.description: The data comprises processed active-source seismic data acquired with land seismometers. The data were collected in the Atacama Desert in connection with expedition SO297 with the research vessel RV SONNE (doi:10.48433/cr_so297) offshore Copiapó, north-central Chile (map for further details). The entire data set consists of 10 land seismometer stations. Seismic data were shot offshore in one profile. Each land station consists of 3 channels (channel 1: horizontal component 1; channel 2: horizontal component 2; channel 3: vertical component).

data.name: Seismic processed data (Ocean Bottom Hydrophone working area dataset) of RV SONNE during cruise SO297, Taltal Ridge, Chile
data.description: The data comprises processed active-source seismic data acquired with ocean bottom hydrophones (OBH). The entire data set consists of 16 OBH stations. Seismic data were shot across the OBH along one profile. Each OBH consists of 1 channel (hydrophone).

data.name: Seismic processed data (Ocean Bottom Seismometer working area dataset) of RV SONNE during cruise SO297, Taltal Ridge, Chile
data.description: The data comprises processed active-source seismic data acquired with ocean bottom seismometer (OBS). The data were collected at the Taltal Ridge during expedition SO297 with the research vessel RV SONNE (doi:10.48433/cr_so297) offshore Taltal, north-central Chile (map for further details). The entire data set consists of 27 OBS stations. Seismic data were shot across the OBS along one profile. Each OBS consists of 4 channels (channel 1: hydrophone; channel 2: horizontal component 1; channel 3: horizontal component 2; channel 4: vertical component).

data.name: Seismic processed data (Onshore Seismometer working area dataset) of project PISAGUA, Atacama Desert near Taltal, Chile
data.description: The data comprises processed active-source seismic data acquired with land seismometers. The data were collected in the Atacama Desert in connection with expedition SO297 with the research vessel RV SONNE (doi:10.48433/cr_so297) offshore Taltal, north-central Chile (map under further details). The entire data set consists of 13 land seismometers. Seismic data were shot offshore in one profile. Each land station consists of 3 channels (channel 1: horizontal component 1; channel 2: horizontal component 2; channel 3: vertical component).

processing.summary: Amphibious wide-angle seismic refraction data from SO297 cruise processed through 2D P-wave travel time tomography. Both OBS/OBH marine instruments and onshore seismic land stations recorded airgun shots along the 445 km transect. Tomographic velocity model derived; local seismicity catalogue compiled from passively recorded events during instrument deployment.
workflow: 3. Phase picking (Pg, PmP, Pn) on OBS/OBH and land station records
5. Seismicity catalogue compilation from passive recording
6. Integration of velocity model with seismicity to define subduction geometry
processing.notes: Amphibious experiment: 36 OBS/OBH + 10 land stations. 12 G-gun array (89 L = 5431 cui), 2089 shots at 60 s intervals (~120 m spacing), towed at 8 m depth. Passive seismicity catalogue compiled during deployment period (December 2022–June 2023).

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF (CC-BY open access). 8 authors confirmed correct. vessel updated to R/V SONNE (SO297). year_acquired corrected 2022→2023 (marine expedition was February–April 2023; land stations deployed December 2022–June 2023). source_volume_cui=5431 (12 G-guns, 89 L total). Software: PasteUp for picking + Seismic Unix. Data not yet deposited in a public repository as of April 2026.
